speeches.name: Jerome Powell
speeches.summary: At the April FOMC press conference, Powell said policy was appropriately positioned and that the Fed was prepared to move in either direction as incoming data evolved.

speeches.name: Jerome Powell
speeches.summary: At the October FOMC press conference, Powell said the Committee cut rates by 25 bps but stressed that a further December cut was not a foregone conclusion.

speeches.name: Jerome Powell
speeches.summary: At the April FOMC press conference, Powell described policy as appropriate to support progress toward the inflation and employment goals while keeping the option to adjust based on data.

speeches.name: Jerome Powell
speeches.summary: Powell's remarks emphasized that the Fed remains data-dependent and balanced between inflation risks and labor-market conditions.

speeches.name: Jerome Powell
speeches.summary: Powell reiterated that the Committee is well positioned to respond either to upside inflation surprises or to weaker labor-market conditions.
